# **Fixing Unmaintained Spark Jobs Unsuitable for Production clusters**

Status: *Under review*  Author: [Yi Chen](mailto:yichen@stripe.com)    Created: Oct 15, 2024  Last Updated: Nov 6, 2024

# Summary

The Spark infrastructure reliability effort entered a new phase in FY25Q3 (see [announcement](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C8EDM9MGA/p1726591997822399)), after addressing most infrastructure reliability changes. In this phase, we are asking job owners to help further improve Spark cluster reliability and cost efficiency.

The first project is to identify and fix Spark jobs that do not meet production quality standards based on their failure rates. We expect jobs to succeed at least 95% of the time within a rolling 7-day window (assuming no known cluster or noisy neighbor issues) to be considered production quality and suitable for a production cluster. As of Nov 6, 2024, less than 3% of all Spark jobs run from Airflow require a retry[\[1\]](#bookmark=id.yqmmv18alrxt). As shown by the data, jobs requiring retries consume a disproportionate share of cluster resources. For example, the task “mentions” of the dag “teams\_champions” ([link](https://airflow.tinyspeck.com/dags/teams_champions/grid?search=teams_champions)) consumed 19% of the prod-etl-emr6 cluster’s resources in the past 7 days alone.

The goal of this project is to identify these unmaintained Spark jobs (3% of all Spark tasks), and find a resolution or a plan by December 31, 2024\. 

# Methodology

We are tracking the list of these jobs using this query based on the YARN metrics: [https://analytics.tinyspeck.com/v3/explore/1000000001494476](https://analytics.tinyspeck.com/v3/explore/1000000001494476)

This query tracks the list of Spark jobs that are failing frequently in the most recent 7 days. The result is reverse ordered by the total memory\_seconds consumption, which can be used to measure the cost savings opportunities of fixing these jobs. 

| Column Name | Meaning |
| :---- | :---- |
| airflow\_dag | Dag ID |
| airflow\_task | Task ID |
| cluster\_name | EMR cluster name |
| total\_memory\_seconds | The sum of memory consumption of this task over the past 7 days, including all retries |
| total\_cluster\_memory\_seconds | The sum of memory consumption of all tasks in this cluster |
| cluster\_usage\_percentage | The percentage of total consumed resource in this cluster by this task |
| max\_airflow\_try\_number | The max number of airflow retries for this task during the last 7 days |
| failed\_runs | The number of failed executions of this task over the past 7 days |
| total\_runs | The total number of executions of this task over the past 7 days |
| failure\_rate | The percentage of the failed executions over the past 7 days. If this number is larger than 5% we don’t consider this job production quality. |

# Desired Outcome

We will manually run the [dashboard](https://analytics.tinyspeck.com/v3/explore/1000000001494476) to filter out potential issues created by cluster reliability issues, and work with the job owners through an iterative process. 

* We will create JIRA tickets for the respective owner teams and mark them according to urgency.  
* We expect the job owner to provide a resolution by November 30\. The resolution can be one of the following:  
  * Set the Airflow retry limit to 1\. This ensures that the job will not be retried by Airflow after a single failure and gives the job owner until the end of 2024 (12/31/24) to implement one of the long-term solutions listed below  
  * Long term solution: delete the job from the infrastructure  
  * Long term solution: fix the job so the failure rate is below 5%

# First Batch of 26 unmaintained Spark jobs (Nov 6, 2024\)

I identified the top 26 unmaintained Spark jobs that could deliver significant benefits if addressed. Please see the details in this spreadsheet: [Fix and Tune Spark Jobs for Spark Infrastructure Reliability](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/142gQcc7XRUxjGCUGLib8vSHKG9UvQW_waBkYr3ZsM7E/edit?gid=620606576#gid=620606576)

The following chart shows the relative priority and opportunity sizing by teams who own these unmaintained jobs.

The chart below highlights the EMR clusters affected by unmaintained jobs, categorized by their respective owners.

# Appendix

## \[1\] As of Nov 6, 2024, less than 2% of all Spark tasks in the past 7 days have a less than 59% success rate, while more than 96% of tasks were successful at its first Airflow run

As of today (Nov 6, 2024), data shows that over the past 7 days, **more than 96% of all Spark jobs have a 100% success rate. Less than 3% of jobs have a success rate below 97%, and fewer than 2% have a success rate below 59%**. Please see the live query here: [https://analytics.tinyspeck.com/v3/explore/1000000001516737](https://analytics.tinyspeck.com/v3/explore/1000000001516737))

![][image3]
